text:
There were once three queens who lived long ago in an enchanted forest. They were fierce rivals, squabbling incessantly.

The first queen grew jealous that no stone flowed into her flowing streams. She decided to turn rivers into mere ditches.

The second queen wanted all the dewdrops from leaves in a single night. She cast a spell, and it rained stars—so abundant that bees forgot their hive duties.

The third queen desired eternal youth. Gathering starlight in her hands, she held it over her head.

"You can't have everything," the stars whispered through the night.

The first queen tried the ditch-stream transformation. But fish simply swam away. Birds did not visit.

The second queen wished for the stars' twinkling beauty. They swirled in the sky, dazzling but insignificant.

The third queen wished for everlasting life. The starlight faded into nothingness.

The forest sighed. The queens wept, lamenting their petty wishes. Then they gathered and laughed.
